summary: No-ship: the slice still has defensible paths to leave the machine-wide hook installed after deselect, delete unrelated user hooks, clobber settings on parse failure, and block SessionStart on a wedged bd.

findings.title: Deselect has no wired path to remove the user-scope hook
findings.recommendation: Wire `scripts/uninstall-hooks.mjs` into the actual deselect/park/uninstall lifecycle, or install this as a tracked hook-group artifact through the shared owner-scoped writer so removal is automatic. Add an integration test that selects, verifies the hook exists, deselects/parks, and proves the owner group is gone from settings.json.

findings.title: Native bd de-dupe can delete unrelated hand-authored hook groups
findings.recommendation: Restrict native de-dupe to the specific untagged `SessionStart` group shape emitted by `bd setup claude`: no owner tag, no non-native hooks, and an exact/normalized `bd prime --hook-json` command. Do not scan every event or drop a group merely because one hook command contains `bd prime`.

findings.title: Malformed existing settings.json is silently replaced on install
findings.recommendation: If an existing settings file cannot be parsed as a non-array object, abort without writing and leave the live bytes untouched. Use the shared/atomic settings writer or an equivalent temp-file rename path, and add regression coverage for invalid JSON/trailing garbage.

findings.title: Fail-open command can still block until Claude's hook timeout
findings.recommendation: Wrap `bd prime --hook-json` in a small fail-open launcher that enforces its own short timeout, validates/captures output, emits empty context on any failure, and exits 0 before Claude Code's hook timeout can fire. Cover with a fake `bd` that sleeps longer than the budget.
